faqs.question: What training is required before my space flight?
faqs.answer: All SpaceWing travelers complete our comprehensive training program. Suborbital flights require a 3-day program covering G-force tolerance, emergency procedures, and spacecraft familiarization. Orbital missions require a 5-day program including zero-G training, space station orientation, and mission simulations. Lunar expeditions include 7 days of intensive training plus a 2-day underwater EVA simulation.

faqs.question: Where does training take place?
faqs.answer: Training is conducted at our SpaceWing Training Centers located in Cape Canaveral, Florida; Mojave, California; and Houston, Texas. International travelers may complete initial training at our partner facilities in Europe, Asia, and Australia before coming to the United States for final preparations.

faqs.question: What is the physical fitness requirement?
faqs.answer: Travelers must be in generally good health. Requirements include: ability to walk 100 meters unassisted, normal blood pressure (or controlled with medication), no recent major surgeries, and ability to withstand 4G forces for 30 seconds. Our medical team conducts a full evaluation, and most healthy adults qualify. Certain conditions may require additional clearance.

faqs.question: Will I experience motion sickness?
faqs.answer: Space motion sickness affects approximately 50% of first-time space travelers. Training includes desensitization techniques, and our spacecraft are equipped with anti-nausea systems. Medication is available for those who need it. Most travelers adapt within the first 24 hours of their mission.

faqs.question: How do you prepare for zero gravity?
faqs.answer: Zero-G training is a highlight of our program! You'll experience weightlessness in our specialized aircraft (parabolic flights), practice moving, eating, and using facilities in zero-G simulators, and learn techniques for working efficiently in microgravity. Our facilities include neutral buoyancy tanks for realistic spacewalking practice.

faqs.question: What should I pack for training?
faqs.answer: SpaceWing provides all training materials, flight suits, and equipment. You should pack: comfortable casual clothing, workout attire, personal toiletries, any prescription medications, and a positive attitude! Training facilities include pools, fitness centers, and relaxation areas. A detailed packing list is provided upon registration.

faqs.question: Can I skip training if I've flown before?
faqs.answer: Returning astronauts who've flown within the past 24 months may qualify for our Express Training program, which reduces training time by 50%. However, spacecraft-specific familiarization is always required. SpaceWing Astronaut Society members with multiple flights receive personalized training paths.

faqs.question: What happens if I fail part of the training?
faqs.answer: Our training team works closely with each traveler to ensure success. If you struggle with any component, additional instruction and practice time are provided at no extra cost. In rare cases where medical or safety concerns arise, you may be offered an alternative mission or a refund according to our policies.

faqs.question: Is training included in the ticket price?
faqs.answer: Yes! All standard training programs are included in your ticket price. This includes accommodation during training (double occupancy for standard cabins, single for premium), all meals, training materials, and transportation between training facilities and the launch site. Luxury accommodations are available for an additional fee.

faqs.question: Can family members attend training?
faqs.answer: Family members and friends can visit during designated visitor hours. We also offer our Family Space Camp program where loved ones can experience simulator rides, tour facilities, and participate in some training activities alongside you. This is a popular option for families wanting to share the journey.
